verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is a type 1 transmembrane protein with an N-terminal signal peptide, a single transmembrane domain, and a C-terminal cytoplasmic region. The protein localizes to the ER membrane under baseline conditions but transits to the plasma membrane during store-operated calcium entry (SOCE), where it couples ER calcium depletion sensors to PM ORAI1 calcium channels. The PM localization is functionally documented and stable during the active SOCE signaling cycle. This represents classical surface biology of a functional TM protein.
